sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 29 - Guideline Concerning Reserves of Companies in Rehabilitation.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 29 - Guideline Concerning Reserves of Companies in Rehabilitation

observedSectionWindows.note: Rehabilitation background, reserve interpretation, issue-date context, expense-allowance context, and the closing boundary note.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 29 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 30 or later guideline files.
Keep the rehabilitation / court-order context explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer includes encoding noise.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Rehabilitation reserve guidance
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the two-page window together and do not widen into AG 30.

proposedBatches.title: AG 29 rehabilitation reserve guidance slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full two-page guideline as one review-only slice because the rehabilitation caveat, issue-date context, and expense-allowance language belong together.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full two-page guideline as one review-only slice because the rehabilitation caveat, issue-date context, and expense-allowance language belong together.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the reserve-interpreting language as regulatory text when the guideline directs how reserves should be interpreted in rehabilitation.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat rehabilitation, issue date, surrender restrictions, CRVM, and CARVM references as terminology or context when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Valuation Law, CRVM, CARVM, and surrender values as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.caveatOrCompanionGuidance: Treat the rehabilitation-specific caveats, court-order framing, and issue-date examples as review-only caveat guidance.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title, scene-setting paragraphs, and explanatory transition language as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1-2 window as a review-only slice and do not split it unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the encoded text layer, rehabilitation-specific reserve interpretation, and issue-date / expense-allowance examples as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 29 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch remains review-only by default.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 29 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag29-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-111 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 29 definition file so the shared runner can find batch-111.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 29 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 29 planning artifact. Planned batch 111 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
